ux_audit.finding: Half-arc gauge with gradient stroke and SVG glow is visually polished but the score label '—' in empty state offers no affordance. The guidance copy is passive ('Your security journey starts here.') and the CTA 'Run First Scan' ranks below the fold on smaller screens.

ux_audit.element: MetricTile cards (Total Scans, Critical, High Risk, Clean Scans)
ux_audit.finding: Animated counter (useAnimatedValue) delivers perceived snappiness, but all four tiles compete equally for attention. Critical count should visually dominate (pulsing border, larger font) because it's the most actionable number. Trend badge only appears if value changed — users with stable critical counts get no reinforcement.

Area chart shows Total/Critical/High but lacks annotations or zero-line callout. Students cannot tell if a downward curve is 'good' or 'bad' without the Learning Tip. Chart legend is 10px tiny text at bottom. Empty state is well-handled but offers no simulated preview.

Donut at 160×160 is undersized and the center is empty — wasted space. Severity progress bars stretch across full width but have no percentage labels. On light mode, yellow-on-white Low bar has contrast ratio ~2.8:1, failing WCAG AA.

Grouping logic is smart (dedup by repo name), but 340px max-height on 5-column feed means only ~5 groups visible. No loading skeleton inside feed. Timeline dot colors depend on severity aggregation but there's no legend — discovery relies on pattern recognition.

Capped at 3 actions (slice(0,3)) with no 'View all' overflow link. Items only surface critical/high from done scans — medium-severity repos never appear, creating false 'All clear' for repos with only medium findings. CTA arrow is hidden until hover — invisible on touch devices.

Skeleton uses pulse animation but layout doesn't match the actual rendered grid perfectly — skeleton Row 1 gauge is col-span-3 but tiles go into a 4-column sub-grid, creating minor CLS on hydration. No shimmer-sweep class applied (defined but unused).

Light mode uses 'bg-white/70 border-white/60' which on the '#f4f6fb' background produces near-invisible card edges (~1.1:1 contrast). Interactive elements (Refresh, feed items) lack visible focus rings in light mode — :focus-visible only shows for keyboard but ring color 'indigo-500/50' is too subtle on light cards.

prioritized_problems.title: Critical count doesn't demand attention
prioritized_problems.why: Users skip past Critical metrics because they look identical to Total Scans and Clean Scans — the red accent is subtle and there's no urgency indicator.

prioritized_problems.title: Empty donut center wastes prime real estate
prioritized_problems.why: The donut center is blank, missing the chance to display grand total or most-severe count — the most glanceable position on the card.

prioritized_problems.title: ActionCard shows false 'All clear' for medium-only repos
prioritized_problems.why: Repos with only medium findings are excluded from actionList, so users with 50 medium vulnerabilities see 'All clear — no urgent actions.'

prioritized_problems.title: Light-mode card borders are invisible
prioritized_problems.why: border-white/60 on bg-[#f4f6fb] yields ~1.1:1 contrast — cards appear to float with no defined edge, breaking visual hierarchy.

prioritized_problems.title: Feed lacks real-time update indicator
prioritized_problems.why: Users can't tell when feed data was last refreshed — there's no timestamp or badge for in-progress scans that auto-update.

prioritized_problems.title: Charts lack contextual annotations
prioritized_problems.why: Students can't interpret trends without the collapsed Learning Tip — inline annotations (e.g., 'Improving ↓12%') would increase chart comprehension.

prioritized_problems.title: Skeleton shimmer-sweep unused, CLS on hydration
prioritized_problems.why: The shimmerSweep CSS class is defined in index.css but never applied to skeleton divs; layout mismatch causes minor cumulative layout shift.

prioritized_problems.title: No keyboard shortcut or quick-scan action from dashboard
prioritized_problems.why: Power users must click 'New Scan' and navigate to /scan — no Cmd+K or shortcut to launch from the dashboard for repeat workflows.

quick_win.description: Add a pulsing red border ring to the Critical MetricTile when value > 0 using 'ring-2 ring-red-500/30 animate-pulse'
quick_win.trade_offs: Pulse animation may feel alarming to some users; disable in prefers-reduced-motion

acceptance_criteria.functional: Critical tile shows pulsing red ring when critical_count > 0, static ring when 0
acceptance_criteria.ux: Eye-tracking priority: Critical tile noticed within 2s of page load in moderated test
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: ring disabled via prefers-reduced-motion: reduce; aria-live='polite' on value change
acceptance_criteria.performance: No additional bundle size; CSS-only animation

recommended.description: Enlarge Critical tile to span 2 columns on lg when count > 0, with prominent count, pulsing border, and inline 'Fix now →' CTA linking to the most-critical scan result
recommended.trade_offs: Grid reflow needed; requires conditional grid layout logic; other tiles compress

acceptance_criteria.functional: Critical tile expands to col-span-2 when critical_count > 0, collapses back when 0
acceptance_criteria.ux: Users identify and click 'Fix now' within 5s in task-based usability test
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: role='alert' on Critical tile when count first increases; sr-only announcement
acceptance_criteria.performance: Grid reflow completes < 16ms (single animation frame)

stretch.description: Animate Critical tile entry with a shake micro-interaction on first load if count > 0, plus a red gradient pulse behind the icon
stretch.trade_offs: May feel aggressive; needs user preference toggle; increased CSS complexity

acceptance_criteria.functional: Shake plays once on mount, not on subsequent re-renders
acceptance_criteria.ux: 80%+ users report noticing Critical count in post-test survey
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Animation fully disabled via prefers-reduced-motion
acceptance_criteria.performance: CSS keyframe only; no JS per-frame computation

quick_win.description: Add centered text inside the PieChart using a custom label: grand total number + 'findings' label
quick_win.trade_offs: Recharts inner label requires absolute positioning or custom SVG text element

acceptance_criteria.functional: Grand total number renders at donut center, updates reactively
acceptance_criteria.ux: Number is legible at 14px+ font weight 800 on both themes
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: aria-label on PieChart SVG includes total count
acceptance_criteria.performance: No additional re-render — pure SVG text element

recommended.description: Replace donut center with a mini radial gauge showing 'worst severity' percentage (Critical / Total). Increase donut size to 200×200. Add hover-to-filter: clicking a segment filters the vulnerability list.
recommended.trade_offs: Click-to-filter requires state lifting to parent or context; larger chart takes more space

acceptance_criteria.functional: Center shows 'X% Critical' or 'X% High' depending on worst; click segment triggers filter
acceptance_criteria.ux: Users can filter by severity from the chart within 3 clicks
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Each Pie segment has aria-label with name and count; focus ring on segments
acceptance_criteria.performance: Recharts onClick handler; no additional API calls

stretch.description: Animate donut segments in sequence on mount (stagger 100ms each), with the center number counting up. Add a radial gradient glow behind the worst-severity segment.
stretch.trade_offs: Complex animation sequencing; may conflict with prefers-reduced-motion

acceptance_criteria.functional: Segments animate in severity-order with 100ms stagger
acceptance_criteria.ux: Animation completes in <800ms total; feels responsive
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: All animations disabled under prefers-reduced-motion
acceptance_criteria.performance: CSS transitions on stroke-dashoffset; no JS animation loop

quick_win.description: Extend actionList logic to include medium-severity repos when no critical/high actions exist, with label 'Review X medium in repo'
quick_win.trade_offs: May surface low-urgency items; mitigate with dimmer visual weight

acceptance_criteria.functional: ActionCard never shows 'All clear' if any medium+ vulnerability exists across done scans
acceptance_criteria.ux: Medium actions use amber accent instead of red/orange; clear visual hierarchy
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: aria-label includes severity level for each action item
acceptance_criteria.performance: No additional API calls; computed from existing jobs array

recommended.description: Three-tier action system: (1) Fix critical, (2) Fix high, (3) Review medium. Show top 4 actions with severity-colored dots. Add 'View all' link when more than 4 exist.
recommended.trade_offs: More complex logic; may create info overload if many repos scanned

acceptance_criteria.functional: Actions sorted by severity, max 4 shown, 'View all' appears when >4
acceptance_criteria.ux: Each action has severity dot + repo name + count + arrow affordance (visible, not hover-only)
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: List uses role='list' with role='listitem'; arrow always visible (not hover-gated)
acceptance_criteria.performance: useMemo with stable dependencies; no polling

stretch.description: AI-generated prioritized action list: use AI explanation engine to produce a 1-sentence recommendation per action (e.g., 'SQL injection in auth.py — patch input validation first').
stretch.trade_offs: Requires AI call latency; cache results; may exceed MAX_AI_ENRICH limit

acceptance_criteria.functional: AI suggestions appear within 5s of dashboard load via cached results
acceptance_criteria.ux: Each action shows AI summary in tooltip on hover/focus
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Tooltip accessible via focus; aria-describedby links action to tooltip
acceptance_criteria.performance: AI results cached by scan job_id MD5; no re-enrichment on page refresh

quick_win.description: Change light-mode border from 'border-white/60' to 'border-slate-200/60' and card bg from 'bg-white/70' to 'bg-white/80' for visible edges
quick_win.trade_offs: Slightly less glass-pure aesthetic; better usability

acceptance_criteria.functional: Card borders visible on #f4f6fb background in light mode
acceptance_criteria.ux: Border contrast ratio >= 1.5:1 against background (non-text element threshold)
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: WCAG 2.1 1.4.11 Non-text Contrast: 3:1 for UI component boundaries
acceptance_criteria.performance: Zero runtime cost; Tailwind class swap

recommended.description: Dual-layer border: subtle solid border 'border-slate-200/50' + inset shadow 'shadow-[inset_0_1px_0_rgba(255,255,255,0.8)]' for depth without heavy lines. Apply consistently to all glass cards.
recommended.trade_offs: Inset shadow adds paint cost; negligible on modern GPUs

acceptance_criteria.functional: All glass cards have visible boundaries in both light and dark mode
acceptance_criteria.ux: Cards look distinct from background without harsh borders
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Meets 3:1 non-text contrast for boundaries
acceptance_criteria.performance: box-shadow is GPU-composited; no layout reflow

stretch.description: Use CSS @supports (backdrop-filter: blur(1px)) to conditionally apply glassmorphism; fallback to solid bg-white + border-slate-200 for devices without GPU compositing.
stretch.trade_offs: More CSS; slight visual difference on fallback devices

acceptance_criteria.functional: Fallback renders correctly on Safari <15, Firefox ESR
acceptance_criteria.ux: Fallback cards look clean and professional, not broken
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: prefers-reduced-transparency media query switches to solid bg
acceptance_criteria.performance: @supports check is zero-cost; fallback avoids GPU compositing entirely

quick_win.description: Add a 'Last updated Xs ago' label below the Live Feed header, computed from fetchJobs timestamp stored in a ref
quick_win.trade_offs: Requires storing last-fetch timestamp; minor state addition

acceptance_criteria.functional: 'Last updated' shows relative time, updates every 30s
acceptance_criteria.ux: Users can verify data freshness without manually refreshing
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: aria-live='polite' on the timestamp for screen reader updates
acceptance_criteria.performance: Single ref + setInterval(30s); negligible

recommended.description: Auto-poll every 30s for job status changes. Show a subtle pulse dot next to 'Live Feed' when in-progress scans exist. New items slide in with fadeInUp animation.
recommended.trade_offs: 30s polling adds network requests; disable when tab not visible (Page Visibility API)

acceptance_criteria.functional: Feed auto-updates; pulse dot visible when any job status is 'running'
acceptance_criteria.ux: New feed items animate in without jarring layout shifts
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: aria-live='polite' region announces new items; no auto-focus steal
acceptance_criteria.performance: Polling paused via visibilitychange listener; max 1 concurrent request

stretch.description: WebSocket connection for real-time feed updates. Badge count on sidebar 'Dashboard' nav item for in-progress scans.
stretch.trade_offs: WebSocket infrastructure needed; fallback to polling if WS unavailable

acceptance_criteria.functional: Feed updates within 2s of backend status change via WebSocket
acceptance_criteria.ux: Sidebar badge shows count of running scans; disappears when all complete
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Badge count announced via aria-label on nav item
acceptance_criteria.performance: Single WS connection; reconnect with exponential backoff

quick_win.description: Add inline change annotation to area chart: compute delta between last two data points and render a small '+X% ↑' or '-X% ↓' badge next to the latest data point
quick_win.trade_offs: Recharts doesn't natively support annotations — use customized dot or reference line

acceptance_criteria.functional: Delta badge renders at the last data point of Total line
acceptance_criteria.ux: Students immediately see if trend is improving or worsening
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Badge has aria-label with trend direction and percentage
acceptance_criteria.performance: Computed in useMemo; single extra SVG text element

recommended.description: Add ReferenceLine at the average value with a label 'Avg: X'. Show trend arrow + percentage change in the chart header. Highlight best and worst scan points with colored dots.
recommended.trade_offs: More visual noise; keep it minimal with 40% opacity for reference line

acceptance_criteria.functional: ReferenceLine shows at average; best/worst dots highlighted
acceptance_criteria.ux: Chart conveys trend direction within 3 seconds of viewing
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: All annotation values included in chart aria-label
acceptance_criteria.performance: ReferenceLine is a native Recharts component; zero overhead

stretch.description: Interactive brush zoom on the area chart (Recharts Brush component) to focus on a time range. AI-generated trend summary tooltip: 'Your security improved 23% this week.'
stretch.trade_offs: Brush adds ~5KB to Recharts; AI summary adds latency

acceptance_criteria.functional: Brush allows selecting 3-10 scan range; AI summary updates
acceptance_criteria.ux: Users can zoom into interesting periods; AI summary is max 15 words
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Brush handles keyboard-accessible via arrow keys
acceptance_criteria.performance: Brush is lazy-loaded; AI summary cached by date range hash

quick_win.description: Apply shimmer-sweep class to skeleton divs and match exact grid dimensions (gauge h-[280px], tiles h-[130px]) to eliminate CLS

acceptance_criteria.functional: Skeleton layout matches rendered layout pixel-for-pixel
acceptance_criteria.ux: Zero perceptible layout shift on data hydration
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Skeleton divs have aria-hidden='true' and role='presentation'
acceptance_criteria.performance: CLS score < 0.01 for dashboard load

recommended.description: Progressive skeleton-to-content transition: fade skeleton out while content fades in (crossfade 300ms). Use shimmer-sweep on each skeleton block.
recommended.trade_offs: Crossfade adds 300ms perceived delay but smoother visual

acceptance_criteria.functional: Crossfade from skeleton to real content with no flash of unstyled content
acceptance_criteria.ux: Transition feels smooth and intentional
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: aria-busy='true' on skeleton container; 'false' when loaded
acceptance_criteria.performance: CSS opacity transition only; no layout reflow

stretch.description: Render skeleton shapes as exact component outlines (SVG silhouettes of gauge, chart axes, etc.) for maximum fidelity
stretch.trade_offs: High implementation cost for marginal UX gain

acceptance_criteria.functional: Skeleton gauge shows arc outline; chart shows axis lines
acceptance_criteria.ux: Users perceive continuity between loading and loaded states
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: All skeleton elements are aria-hidden
acceptance_criteria.performance: Static SVG; no animation cost beyond shimmer

quick_win.description: Add keyboard shortcut Cmd/Ctrl+K to focus a hidden quick-action bar that offers 'New Scan' and 'Refresh' options
quick_win.trade_offs: May conflict with browser search; use Cmd+Shift+K as alternative

acceptance_criteria.functional: Cmd+K opens dropdown with 2 actions; Esc closes; arrow keys navigate
acceptance_criteria.ux: Power users can trigger scan in 2 keystrokes from dashboard
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Command palette uses role='listbox' with aria-activedescendant
acceptance_criteria.performance: Lazy-mounted component; zero bundle cost until activated

recommended.description: Full command palette (K-bar) with search: New Scan, Refresh, Go to History, Go to Results, Toggle Theme, Toggle Student Mode. Renders as modal overlay.

acceptance_criteria.functional: All 6 commands work; fuzzy search filters list
acceptance_criteria.ux: Opens in <100ms; results filter on each keystroke
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: role='dialog' aria-modal='true'; traps focus; Esc closes
acceptance_criteria.performance: Lazy loaded via React.lazy(); global listener is single addEventListener

stretch.description: AI-assisted command: type 'show me critical findings' and the palette navigates to filtered results
stretch.trade_offs: NLP parsing adds complexity; may misinterpret free text

acceptance_criteria.functional: 5 predefined natural language commands recognized
acceptance_criteria.ux: AI responses appear within 200ms (local regex, not API)
acceptance_criteria.accessibility: Results announced via aria-live
acceptance_criteria.performance: Pure client-side regex matching; no API calls

props.topAction: { label: string, jobId: string, severity: string } | null

aria.svg_role: img with aria-label describing score, status, and trend
aria.keyboard: CTA button focusable and activatable via Enter/Space

visual_states.idle: Full gauge arc drawn, score displayed, trend badge visible
visual_states.loading: Skeleton arc placeholder with shimmer-sweep, '—' text
visual_states.error: Gauge shows 0, guidance text: 'Unable to calculate score', orange warning icon
visual_states.empty: Gauge track visible with no fill, '—' score, CTA 'Run First Scan' prominent

animation_spec.prefers_reduced_motion: All animations replaced with instant state via animation-duration: 0.01ms

unit_tests: GIVEN score=null THEN renders '—' text and 'Run First Scan' CTA button
GIVEN score=85, previousScore=72 THEN renders TrendingUp icon with +13 badge
GIVEN prefers-reduced-motion=reduce THEN score renders immediately without animation frame loop

visual_states.error: Hidden — component not rendered if jobs fail to load

visual_states.error: Inline warning: 'Unable to load recent activity'
visual_states.empty: Zap icon + 'No scan activity yet' + CTA

animation_spec.pulse_dot: animate-pulse 2s ease-in-out infinite for in-progress scans

GIVEN group with 3 items THEN shows ×3 badge and expands on click

micro_interactions.name: Metric counter cascade
micro_interactions.description: All MetricTile values animate from 0 to final count using useAnimatedValue hook with requestAnimationFrame
micro_interactions.disable_when: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — values render instantly via early return in useAnimatedValue

micro_interactions.name: Card hover lift
micro_interactions.trigger: Mouse enter on any glass card
micro_interactions.timing: 250ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1) for transform, 250ms ease for box-shadow
micro_interactions.description: translateY(-2px) via .card-hover class. No scale change to avoid text blur.
micro_interactions.disable_when: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — transform:none via CSS media query; also disable on touch-primary devices via @media (hover: none)

micro_interactions.name: Gauge arc draw
micro_interactions.description: Half-arc gauge draws from 0% to score percentage with SVG stroke-dasharray. Glow filter tracks the arc endpoint.
micro_interactions.disable_when: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — set stroke-dasharray immediately via transition-duration:0ms

micro_interactions.name: Feed group expand/collapse
micro_interactions.description: Sub-items fade in (opacity 0→1) and expand (max-height 0→2000px). Chevron rotates 180°.
micro_interactions.disable_when: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — instant expand with no animation

micro_interactions.name: Stagger children entrance
micro_interactions.trigger: Dashboard section enters viewport (or on mount)
micro_interactions.description: Each card in a grid row fades in and translates up 16px. Applied via .stagger-children class.
micro_interactions.disable_when: prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — all children render at full opacity with no transform

color_tokens.rule: WCAG 2.1 AA requires 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text and UI components

issues.applies_to: LEARNING_TIPS expanded text, chart axis labels

roles_and_landmarks: main: <main aria-label='Security Dashboard'> wraps entire page
region: HealthGauge, ActionCard, ThreatLandscape, SeverityMatrix, ScanAnalytics each have role='region' + aria-label
feed: Live Feed uses role='feed' with aria-live='polite'
list/listitem: ActionCard actions use role='list' + role='listitem'
img: Gauge SVG uses role='img' with descriptive aria-label including score, status, and trend

aria_live_usage.example: Screen reader announces: '5' when critical count value updates
Screen reader announces: 'New scan completed for my-repo, 3 findings'

keyboard_behavior: All buttons and interactive elements focusable via Tab in DOM order
Feed groups: Enter/Space to expand or navigate; aria-expanded tracks state
Feed sub-items: Tab within expanded group; Enter to view results
Charts: Tab to chart container, native Recharts keyboard nav for data points
focus-visible ring: 'focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-indigo-500/50' on all interactive elements
reduced_motion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) in index.css disables all animations
useAnimatedValue hook returns target immediately when prefersReducedMotion() is true
All CSS animations reset to animation-duration:0.01ms, animation-iteration-count:1
Transforms forced to none for card-hover, stagger-children, float orbs

performance_plan.task: Apply shimmer-sweep to SkeletonDashboard, fix grid dimensions to match rendered layout
performance_plan.detail: Add shimmer-sweep class to all skeleton divs. Match gauge col-span-3 h-[280px], tiles col-span-6 grid-cols-2 h-[130px], actionCard col-span-3. Measure CLS < 0.01.

performance_plan.task: Cache jobs in sessionStorage for instant render on revisit
performance_plan.detail: Already implemented — useState initializer reads sessionStorage. Verify TTL (currently unlimited — add 5min expiry).
Wrap AreaChart/BarChart/PieChart imports in React.lazy(). Show skeleton chart placeholder during load. Saves ~45KB from initial bundle.

performance_plan.task: AI enrichment: cache by job_id MD5, concurrency limit = 3, MAX_AI_ENRICH = 5
performance_plan.detail: Backend already caches AI results per scan. Frontend should: (1) skip re-fetching AI for reports already in memory, (2) limit concurrent AI poll intervals to 3, (3) only enrich top 5 vulnerabilities per report.

performance_plan.task: Snippet trim to ±5 lines for vulnerability code previews
performance_plan.detail: VulnerabilityCard currently renders full code snippet from backend. Trim to ±5 lines around affected line; add 'Show more' toggle for full context.

performance_plan.task: Page Visibility API: pause polling when tab is hidden
performance_plan.detail: Add visibilitychange listener in DashboardPage to pause/resume auto-refresh interval. Also apply to ScanProgress polling.
@supports not (backdrop-filter: blur(1px)) { apply solid bg-[#161929] dark / bg-white light }. Also add @media (prefers-reduced-transparency: reduce) fallback.
Feed currently renders up to 15 groups × N items. If users accumulate 100+ scans, virtualize with react-window or CSS content-visibility:auto.

empty_states.chart_trend: Run a scan to see trends.

kpis.name: Time to first fix action
kpis.definition: Median time from dashboard load to clicking an ActionCard action button

kpis.name: AI-explain completion rate
kpis.definition: Percentage of scan reports where AI analysis completes within 2 minutes

kpis.name: Dashboard bounce rate
kpis.definition: Users who leave within 5s without any interaction

moderated_tasks.task: You just opened SecureTrail for the first time. Without clicking anything, tell me what you think this dashboard is showing you.
moderated_tasks.success_criteria: Participant correctly identifies security score, vulnerability counts, and scan activity within 30 seconds
moderated_tasks.measurement: Think-aloud observation; time to first correct interpretation

moderated_tasks.task: You have 3 critical vulnerabilities. Find and start fixing the most urgent one.
moderated_tasks.success_criteria: Participant clicks ActionCard 'Fix critical' button or navigates via gauge CTA within 15 seconds
moderated_tasks.measurement: Task completion rate; clicks to complete; time on task

moderated_tasks.task: Is your code getting more or less secure over time? How can you tell?
moderated_tasks.success_criteria: Participant references Threat Landscape chart or gauge trend badge within 20 seconds
moderated_tasks.measurement: Accuracy of trend interpretation; use of Learning Tip

moderated_tasks.task: Find the results of your last scan and export them.
moderated_tasks.success_criteria: Participant navigates to results from Live Feed and clicks Export within 30 seconds
moderated_tasks.measurement: Task completion rate; navigation path chosen

moderated_tasks.task: Toggle Student Mode off. What changed? Which mode do you prefer and why?
moderated_tasks.success_criteria: Participant finds toggle in sidebar and articulates at least 2 differences
moderated_tasks.measurement: Discovery time; qualitative preference and reasoning

unmoderated_tasks: Identify the overall security score of your repositories
Find how many critical vulnerabilities exist across all scans
Determine which severity level has the most findings
Start a new security scan from the dashboard
Refresh the dashboard data
Navigate to a completed scan result from the Live Feed
Expand a grouped scan entry in the Live Feed
Interpret whether vulnerabilities are trending up or down
Find the Next Steps card and click on an action item
Open a Learning Tip on any card
Switch between dark and light mode
Toggle Student Mode on or off from the sidebar
Navigate to scan history from the dashboard
Identify which scanner (Semgrep, Trivy, Gitleaks) found the most issues
Find a scan that had zero vulnerabilities (clean scan)
Read the severity breakdown in the Severity Matrix
Hover over a data point in the Threat Landscape chart to see details
Find the job ID of the most recent scan
Determine if any scan is currently in progress
Use the bar chart to compare findings between two scans

recruitment_profile.primary: Computer science students (2nd-4th year), bootcamp graduates, junior developers (<2 years experience)
recruitment_profile.secondary: Mentors, hackathon judges, security-aware senior developers
recruitment_profile.sample_size: 8-12 participants per round (5 moderated, 20 unmoderated per cycle)
recruitment_profile.criteria: Must have used at least one code editor or IDE; no prior SecureTrail experience required

post_task_questions: On a scale of 1-5, how easy was it to understand the security posture of your code?
On a scale of 1-5, how confident are you about what to do next?
On a scale of 1-5, how overwhelming did the dashboard feel?
On a scale of 1-5, how would you rate the visual clarity of the charts?
On a scale of 1-5, how useful is Student Mode for learning about security?
What was the most confusing element on the dashboard?
What was the single most useful piece of information?
Would you recommend this tool to a classmate? (1-5)
How would you describe this tool to a friend in one sentence?
What feature would you add or change first?

ab_tests.name: Critical tile emphasis
ab_tests.hypothesis: Enlarging the Critical MetricTile (col-span-2 with pulsing border) when critical_count > 0 will increase ActionCard click-through rate by 20%
ab_tests.variant: Critical tile expands to col-span-2 with ring-2 ring-red-500/30 animate-pulse when critical > 0
ab_tests.sample_size: ~500 sessions per variant (alpha=0.05, power=0.8, baseline CTR=25%, MDE=5pp)

ab_tests.name: Donut center label vs no label
ab_tests.hypothesis: Adding grand total count + 'findings' to the donut center will reduce time-to-comprehend severity distribution by 30%
ab_tests.primary_metric: Time from dashboard load to first severity-related interaction (filter click or chart hover)
ab_tests.variant: Grand total in center: bold number + 'findings' label
ab_tests.sample_size: ~400 sessions per variant (continuous metric, two-sample t-test)

ab_tests.name: Auto-expand Learning Tips in Student Mode
ab_tests.hypothesis: Showing Learning Tips expanded by default (instead of collapsed) will increase learning_tip_opened rate by 40% and improve SUS score by 5 points
ab_tests.control: Learning Tips collapsed by default (click to expand)
ab_tests.variant: First Learning Tip on gauge auto-expanded; others collapsed

handoff_deliverables.description: 4 frames: Dashboard (dark+light), empty state (dark+light), each with glassmorphism card components as reusable instances
JSON file mapping token names (glass-bg-dark, glass-border-dark, glass-shadow-dark, etc.) to exact Tailwind classes for consistency across pages
Stories for: HealthGauge, MetricTile, ActionCard, FeedGroup, SeverityBar, LearningTip, ChartTooltip, SkeletonDashboard — each with idle/loading/error/empty states + dark/light/student variants
axe-core scan results + manual WCAG 2.1 AA checklist covering color contrast, keyboard navigation, screen reader announcements, and reduced-motion compliance
Jest + React Testing Library tests for all 6 component specs (3 tests each = 18 tests), plus 5 integration tests for dashboard data flow
Lighthouse CI report: LCP, CLS, FID baseline pre/post implementation. Target: LCP < 2s, CLS < 0.01, FID < 100ms

week_1.title: Foundation & P0 Fixes
milestones: Fix light-mode border contrast (P4) — Frontend, 0.5d
Fix skeleton CLS + apply shimmer-sweep (P7) — Frontend, 0.5d
Add donut center label (P2 quick-win) — Frontend, 0.5d
Extend ActionCard to include medium severity (P3 quick-win) — Frontend, 0.5d
Add Critical tile pulsing border (P1 quick-win) — Frontend, 0.5d
SessionStorage TTL (5min expiry) — Frontend, 0.25d
Tailwind design token JSON created — UX, 0.5d

risks.risk: Light-mode border change may affect other pages
risks.mitigation: Audit all glass cards across scan/results/scanning pages

week_2.title: Charts & Feed Enhancement
milestones: ThreatLandscape ReferenceLine + delta annotation (P6) — Frontend, 1d
Donut upsized to 200×200 with center summary (P2 recommended) — Frontend, 1d
Live Feed auto-refresh with visibility API (P5 + PERF-6) — Frontend, 1d
Feed 'last updated' timestamp + running pulse dot — Frontend, 0.5d
Lazy-load Recharts via React.lazy (PERF-3) — Frontend, 1d

risks.risk: Recharts lazy-loading may cause flash on slow connections
risks.mitigation: Use Suspense with skeleton chart fallback

week_3.title: AI Caching & Performance
milestones: AI result caching by job_id MD5, MAX_AI_ENRICH=5, concurrency=3 (PERF-4) — Backend, 2d
Snippet trim to ±5 lines (PERF-5) — Frontend, 0.5d
Glassmorphism @supports fallback (PERF-7) — Frontend, 1d
ActionCard arrow always visible on touch (P3 fix) — Frontend, 0.25d
Storybook stories for 6 dashboard components — Frontend, 1.5d

risks.risk: AI caching invalidation logic may be complex
risks.mitigation: Cache by immutable job_id — results don't change post-scan

week_4.title: Testing & Polish
milestones: Unit tests for 6 component specs (18 tests) — Frontend, 2d
axe-core accessibility audit + fixes — QA, 1d
Lighthouse CI baseline + optimization pass — Frontend, 1d
Moderated usability test (5 participants) — UX, 2d
Bug fixes from testing — Frontend, 1d

risks.risk: Usability test findings may require design changes
risks.mitigation: Reserve 1d buffer in week 4 for quick-win fixes

exec_summary: The SecureTrail Dashboard audit identified 8 prioritized UX problems affecting student comprehension and engagement, with P1 Critical tile visibility and P2 donut center waste being highest-impact. The 4-week roadmap delivers: (Week 1) contrast/layout/action fixes — 3 dev-days; (Week 2) chart annotations, lazy loading, and live feed polish — 4.5 dev-days; (Week 3) AI caching, snippet trimming, Storybook — 5.25 dev-days; (Week 4) testing, accessibility audit, and usability validation — 7 dev-days. Total estimated effort: ~20 dev-days across Frontend (16d), Backend (2d), UX (1.5d), and QA (1d). Expected outcomes: time-to-first-fix action < 8s (from estimated 15s+), ActionCard CTR > 25%, AI completion rate > 90%, CLS < 0.01, and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance across all dashboard cards.
